title: Manage versioned deterministic rules
description: Create, inspect, activate, retire, and replace attributable immutable Classification Rule versions and Rule Set Versions. Implements G2, G4, and UC-CLASSIFY-004.
rationale: Reusable owner decisions remain safe only when rule scope and lifecycle are explicit, reviewable, and reversible.
story_want: manage deterministic classification rules as explicit immutable versions
story_so_that: reusable behavior can change without losing provenance or rewriting history
acceptance_criteria: Given a supported predicate over schema-allowed fields and an active category identifier, when a rule draft is saved, then CLASSIFY records a new immutable draft version with owner, reason, scope, normalization version, and proposed category.
Given a rule draft is saved, when the operation completes, then no rule or Rule Set Version is activated and no LEDGER mutation occurs.
Given an unsupported field or predicate, when a rule draft is saved, then CLASSIFY returns stable field-level validation errors and creates no activatable version.
Given a category identifier is missing from the current catalogue, when a rule draft is saved, then CLASSIFY returns a stable category-not-found error and creates no activatable version.
Given a category identifier is archived in the current catalogue, when a rule draft is saved, then CLASSIFY returns a stable category-inactive error and creates no activatable version.

Given a draft whose required private validation passed, when the owner explicitly activates it, then CLASSIFY creates a new immutable active Rule Set Version containing the exact activated rule version.
Given an active rule, when the owner retires it with a reason, then CLASSIFY creates a new active Rule Set Version without that rule and retains the retired version for inspection.

Given an active rule that must be narrowed or replaced, when the owner activates a validated successor, then the successor references the prior version and the prior version becomes retired or superseded without in-place mutation.

Given a category rename that preserves the same active category identifier, when rules are inspected, then their category identity is unchanged and their display name resolves to the current catalogue name.
Given any owner decision, correction, or feedback event, when CLASSIFY processes it, then no rule version or Rule Set Version is activated automatically.
security_criteria: Rule inspection and lifecycle diagnostics must not expose normalized tokens or private examples outside the explicit owner-requested data result.
